page: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server configured with two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing a total of 80 GB of VRAM. The host system is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 24 dedicated vCPUs, derived from 12 physical cores operating at 2.2 GHz with a 38 MB L3 cache. It features 170.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 7.08 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear division between CPU-bound tasks and accelerator-driven workloads. Single-core CPU performance is positioned in the bottom 25% across stress-ng, Geekbench, and single-threaded compression tests. Multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall within the middle 50% average tier. However, the server excels in GPU-centric tasks, achieving top-tier performance in the top 10% for large language model (LLM) prompt processing across 135M, 7B, and 70B models, as well as text generation for 7B and 70B models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the hardware profile is heavily weighted toward high-density GPU resources rather than raw CPU compute power. The tradeoff of lower single-core CPU performance is offset by the massive parallel processing capabilities of the dual A100 GPUs. This configuration is optimized for deep learning, LLM inference, and data science workloads that offload computation to the GPU, while it is less optimal for CPU-bound sequential processing or local storage-heavy database tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for GPU-intensive workloads. It features two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 80 GB of total VRAM, paired with an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor offering 24 dedicated vCPUs (12 physical cores) running at 2.2 GHz. The system includes 170.0 GB of RAM but lacks local storage. Benchmark data shows that while single-core CPU performance is in the bottom 25%, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are average. Crucially, the server delivers top-tier performance in the top 10% for large language model (LLM) prompt processing and text generation across medium and large models. This profile aligns with machine learning inference, deep learning, and parallel GPU computing, whereas it is less optimal for sequential CPU-bound tasks.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 2 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 24 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 170 GB RAM, delivering top-tier performance for large language model inference.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-2g features 2 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 24 vCPUs, and 170 GB RAM, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform featuring dual GPUs and high-capacity memory, engineered for top-tier large language model inference and parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: Equipped with two NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs providing 80 GB of total VRAM.
Features 24 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 170 GB of system memory.
Delivers top-tier performance in the top 10% for LLM prompt processing and text generation.
No local storage is bundled, requiring external storage attachments.
Optimized for deep learning, machine learning inference, and GPU-accelerated workloads.
